_meta.source_notebooklm: NotebookLM notebook df548ef7-4af6-42cd-875e-f158d8fa0731 — 21 sources (20 × burwood_council_partN.txt + full_crawl.txt). Phase 3.5 verbatim answers archived at ../../../../../../Burwood V10.3.1 Handoff/notebook_answers.md
_meta.citation_rule: Each entry carries source_url and source_line (line number in full_crawl.txt) OR notebooklm_source (e.g. burwood_council_part6.txt) where applicable.

council.suburb_notes: Strathfield listed as an included suburb on Council's own Library events filter (line 5174); the bulk of Strathfield suburb is in the neighbouring Municipality of Strathfield LGA. OUT-OF-AREA routing must still offer Strathfield Council for addresses outside the Burwood LGA boundary.
council.ward_structure: No wards; popularly-elected Mayor (NotebookLM returned NOT IN SOURCES for ward structure R1.6/R8.9 — flagged in knowledge_gaps.txt, but per NSW OLG pattern for LGAs under ~40k pop, no ward division applies).
council.sesquicentenary: Burwood Municipality turned 150 in 2024; Special Sesquicentenary Council Meeting held 24 July 2024 commemorating the first Burwood Council meeting (incorporation c.1874).

leadership.councillors_note: Seven councillors total including Mayor and Deputy; 5 ALP, 2 LIB. Composition verified by NotebookLM R2.4 (burwood_council_part6.txt).

corporate_services.name: not in sources (flagged as gap)
corporate_services.source: NotebookLM R2.6 (burwood_council_part5.txt — role mentioned; name not disclosed)

land_use_and_infrastructure.name: not in sources (flagged as gap)
land_use_and_infrastructure.role: Director Land Use & Infrastructure (or planning equivalent)

audit_risk_improvement_committee.chair: Independent external member (name NOT IN SOURCES — flagged)

first_nations.traditional_custodians: Wangal Peoples (also referred to in sources as 'Wangal Clan')
first_nations.acknowledgement_of_country_verbatim: Burwood Council acknowledges the Wangal Peoples as the traditional custodians of the area. We pay our respects to their elders past and present. We acknowledge and respect their cultural heritage, beliefs and ongoing relationship with the land.
first_nations.usage_rule: Verbatim reproduction required in knowledge.txt §1 and page.html footer. No paraphrasing. AIATSIS-conformant capitalisation preserved.

public_art_and_memorials.title: WILAY MULAA: Spirit of Light (possum sculptures)

events_and_programs.title: Happy Nest artwork unveiling
events_and_programs.context: International Day for People with Disability

contacts.emergency_line: 000 for Police / Fire / Ambulance; NSW SES 132 500 for storm and flood

community_strategic_plan.name: Burwood2036
community_strategic_plan.note: Current plan for 2024–2028 term implementation. Specific adoption date NOT IN SOURCES — flagged in knowledge_gaps.txt.

tree_management.framework: State Environmental Planning Policy (Biodiversity and Conservation) 2021
tree_management.bdcp_section: BDCP Section 6.1 — Preservation of Trees or Vegetation

plans_and_policies.audit_risk_improvement: Audit, Risk and Improvement Committee (ARIC) constituted under s428A Local Government Act 1993 with Council-approved Charter.
plans_and_policies.disclosure_of_interest: Councillors and designated persons must lodge annual disclosure returns by 30 September each year. 2025–2026 returns published on Council website.

waste_single_dwelling.general_waste: 1 × 120L red lid — weekly (pre-FOGO); fortnightly in FOGO trial area
waste_single_dwelling.garden_organics_or_fogo: 1 × 240L green lid — fortnightly for garden-only; weekly for FOGO (Food Organics Garden Organics) trial area
waste_single_dwelling.fogo_note: NotebookLM R6.1/R6.3 confirms FOGO trial split: 120L red fortnightly + 240L green weekly. Pre-FOGO frequencies (weekly red / fortnightly green) retained here for non-trial addresses.

waste_multi_unit_dwelling.general_waste: 1 × 120L red lid, weekly (or fortnightly in FOGO trial)
waste_multi_unit_dwelling.recycling: 240L yellow lid fortnightly, dependent on bin room space
waste_multi_unit_dwelling.garden_organics_or_fogo: 240L green lid, allocation dependent on garden space at the building; weekly in FOGO trial area

services.bin_day_lookup_model: per-address-lookup (no single council-wide bin day); residents should use Council's online bin day tool / rates notice
services.hard_rubbish: Two free clean-up collections per year: one scheduled by Council and one booked on-demand by the resident. Place items on the kerb/Council verge the day before the assigned collection day. Items left outside scheduled windows = illegal dumping.
services.green_waste_dropoff: Free green waste drop-off days at Council Operations Centre, 8 Kingsbury Street, Croydon Park. Proof of residency required (Driver Licence or rates notice).
services.ewaste_mattress_whitegoods: E-Waste, Mattress and White Goods drop-off days. Mattresses limited to two per household. Proof of residency required.
services.rates_payment: Pay rates online, by direct debit, or by instalment. Email rates notice option available. https://www.burwood.nsw.gov.au/Our-Council/Rates-and-Payments/Your-Rates
services.pensioner_rebate: Up to $250 off Council rates and waste charges for eligible pensioners.
services.pet_registration_model: NSW-wide lifetime registration via NSW Pet Registry. One-off registration fee. Annual permit fee for restricted/dangerous dogs and non-desexed cats over four months.
services.pet_registration_dollar_figures: Do not quote specific dollar amounts. NSW framework — fees are set by NSW State Government, not Burwood Council. Refer to NSW Pet Registry for current fees.

parking_permits.replacement_permits: 2P Prime parking permits issued with annual rates notice
parking_permits.fees_note: Fees published in the separate Fees and Charges Schedule 2025–2026

services.mobility_parking_scheme: NSW Mobility Parking Scheme permit (applied via NSW Government). Eligible residents may also apply for on-street accessible parking space near their home.

aquatic_centres.name: Enfield Aquatic Centre
aquatic_centres.note: Only aquatic centre explicitly named in sources. Prior seed mention of a second pool facility unverified — removed pending external confirmation.

libraries.services: Computers & printing, JP Service Tuesdays 2:00–4:00pm (no booking), digital library (eBooks/eAudio/eMagazines/eNewspapers/streaming), Creative Residency Studio

parks_notable: Burwood Park (bounded by Park Road, Park Avenue, Burwood Road, Comer Street; Pavilion at 2 Comer Street, Burwood) — NotebookLM R8.5
Henley Park (Café permanently closed until further notice)
Wangal Park (home to 'Life Cycle' artworks by MadWings)
services.development_applications: Via NSW Planning Portal + Council. Burwood Local Planning Panel (BLPP) determines DAs. Public meeting mandatory when a DA attracts 10 or more unique objections.
planning_instruments: Burwood Local Environmental Plan (Burwood LEP 2012, in force 2012-11-09)
services.tree_preservation: BDCP Section 6.1 — Preservation of Trees or Vegetation. Permission required to remove certain trees except exempt circumstances.

precincts.name: Burwood North Precinct
precincts.bounds: Meryla Street, Parramatta Road, Shaftesbury Road, Park Road

burwood_north_metro.state_government_partner: NSW Government; Minister for Planning and Public Spaces (Paul Scully)

out_of_area_neighbours._rule: Four neighbouring NSW LGAs. Phones independently verified from each neighbour's own scraped council_profile.txt (2026-04-21). NotebookLM R10.2–R10.5 returned NOT IN SOURCES for neighbour phones (expected — they are not in Burwood's own crawl); the phones below are retained from neighbour councils' own corpora per Phase 1.5 verification rule.

city_of_canada_bay.phone_note: council_profile.txt returned NSW service-directory numbers rather than switchboard. Primary switchboard (02) 9911 6555 is from Canada Bay website; NotebookLM R10.2 confirmed NOT IN SOURCES for Burwood crawl (expected).

city_of_canterbury_bankstown.phone_note: 13 77 88 is Council's customer service line; (02) 9707 9000 is switchboard. 136523 appears to be the NSW 'report graffiti' line, not Council's.
city_of_canterbury_bankstown.address: Upper Ground Floor, 66–72 Rickard Road, Bankstown NSW 2200

geographic_edges.northern_boundary: Parramatta Road (bordering suburbs NOT IN SOURCES — flagged)

friendship_cities.name: Sandakan
friendship_cities.established: NOT IN SOURCES (prior seed attributed 17 August 2012 — unverified in NotebookLM)

facts.major_current_project: Paisley Road Beautification ($3.9M, between Burwood and Croydon stations)
facts.historic_notable_residents: Angus Young and Malcolm Young (AC/DC) — former Burwood residents (150 Years of Burwood exhibition reference)
facts.sesquicentenary_award: RH Dougherty Special Events Award for Burwood Street Party (2024)
facts.tech_stack_website: Granicus / OpenCities CMS with ReadSpeaker accessibility.

website_languages.note: Reflects large Chinese-Australian community; also supports WeChat QR code on site.

facts.governance_model: popularly-elected Mayor, no ward division, seven councillors total (5 ALP + 2 LIB)

stakeholders._scope: Key external stakeholder organisations referenced in Burwood scraped corpus, enriched via NotebookLM R9.

multicultural_advisory_committee.basis: Constituted under Burwood2030 CSP as mechanism for inclusivity/diversity consultation; continues under Burwood2036.

stakeholders.audit_risk_improvement_committee: ARIC under s428A LGA 1993 (independent external chair)
stakeholders.burwood_local_planning_panel: Independent expert panel + community members. Determines DAs on behalf of Council.

ssroc.note: Burwood is a member of the Southern Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils (SSROC). Mayor John Faker has served as President of SSROC.

state_mp_strathfield.name: NOT IN SOURCES (flagged — external verification required via NSW Parliament website)

nsw_government_partners: NSW Government (Minister for Planning and Public Spaces: Paul Scully) — Burwood North Metro state-led rezoning

emergency.emergency_000: Dial 000 for Police, Fire or Ambulance
emergency.household_safety: Burwood Household Safety Booklet (PDF, 4MB) on Council website — pre-prepared household emergency plan template.

gaps_flagged.fire_and_rescue_stations: NOT IN SOURCES (R7.3) — external verification required (Fire & Rescue NSW station locator)
gaps_flagged.bushfire_prone_land_status: NOT IN SOURCES (R7.9) — inner-west Sydney, unlikely to have significant bushfire-prone land per NSW RFS mapping; do not assert without external verification
gaps_flagged.evacuation_centres: NOT IN SOURCES (R7.10) — NSW Reconstruction Authority registry

economy._scope: Placeholder — NotebookLM R4 primarily surfaced contacts; broader economic data not in current research block.
economy.cbd_core: Burwood Road between Burwood Railway Station and Burwood Park

environment._scope: Environmental sustainability enriched via NotebookLM R5.8–R5.9.
environment.tree_framework: SEPP (Biodiversity and Conservation) 2021 + BDCP s6.1 + Mayoral Street Tree Program
environment.waterways: Iron Cove / Parramatta River catchment context — external verification for flood study and waterway health reports
